title: maytenus gracilipes subsp. arguta
intro: Maytenus gracilipes subsp. arguta, also known as the Argentine mayten, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae, native to southern Argentina and Chile. The plant has been found to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Maytenus gracilipes subsp. arguta can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to leverage its health benefits.
